channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: STALKER 2: Cost of Hope expansion revealed for this summer, taking you back to Chornobyl Nuclear Power Plant
item.content:encoded:
STALKER 2: Cost of Hope has been revealed, the first major expansion to STALKER 2 set to arrive across Xbox Series X|S, PlayStation 5 and PC this summer.

Cost of Hope will invite you back into the Zone, weaving a new chapter around the battle between Duty and Freedom. These two rival factions see the Zone as a threat that needs to be contained and a gift that needs to be explored and harnessed for good, and now the tentative peace between them has come to an end.

You’ll continue to play as Skif, the protagonist from STALKER 2, with the Cost of Hope DLC fed into the open world, freeform experience of the main game. A signal on the PDA will trigger the new storyline, and allows you to head off and dip in and out of events.

That’s because this DLC will take you to two new regions to explore: the Chornobyl Nuclear Power Plant that is now calling stalkers back in, and the Iron Forest, which is a maze of paths and uncharted locations. Each region has its own hub, quests and activities, while there’s new weapons and gear to stock up on.

GSC Game Worlds describe this as the “middle chapter of the ‘second trilogy'”, though where the first STALKER was three separate games, the story being told here will all be included in the same game. A future DLC story expansion will wrap up the tale.

STALKER 2: Heart of Chornobyl Review

In our STALKER 2: Heart of Chornobyl review, Miguel said, “STALKER 2: Heart of Chornobyl is an immersive experience like no other. It’s a tense shooter, a gripping survival experience, an immersive explorable sandbox, and a stunning piece of art come to life despite the harrowing circumstances of the team behind it all.”

Source: press release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/03/26/stalker-2-cost-of-hope-expansion-revealed-for-this-summer-taking-you-back-to-chornobyl-nuclear-power-plant/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Expanse: Osiris Reborn is coming in spring 2027, but there’s a closed beta next month
item.content:encoded:
Owlcat Games’ ambitious looking sci-fi action RPG The Expanse: Osiris Reborn is set to launch in spring 2027, the studio has announced, coming to PS5, Xbox Series X|S, PC and set to be a day one launch into Xbox Game Pass.

But, and this is quite a big but, you could play it much sooner than that with a closed beta set to take place next month from 22nd April for those who pre-order the Miller’s Pack or Collector’s Edition.

The beta will include a full mission from the start of the game, set on the Pinkwater 4 station. Here you’ll meet some of its residents, including Zafar who will join your crew, and it will build up to fighting a major enemy and becoming a ship captain. It will be available to play across PC, Xbox Series X|S and PlayStation 5 – wherever it is that you pre-order – but Owlcat naturally explains that performance will be variable at this time and it’s still very much a work in progress.

Best known for their CRPGs Pathfinder: Kingmaker and Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader, Owlcat is shifting gears for their adaptation of The Expanse. The above gameplay trailer shows a lot of snippets of action, much of which has that Mass Effect feel for the dialogue, for the third person combat, and more.

Cast as a Pinkwater Security mercenary, you get wrapped up in the events that ripple through the solar system with the start of the story in the books and TV series, running in parallel to the adventures of the crew of the Rocinante . You can choose to be from Earth and Mars, or be a Belter. You will need to use different skills in combat and outside of it. In combat, the action will be that of a third person shooter but with tactical elements as you will need to use tactical items and the skills of your crew to get the upper hand. The crew will be incredibly important to the story and the way events can unfold. Each person you pick up has their own goals, motivations, and history to unpick, which you may discover while living together on the ship, which is called Gemini. Uncovering the layers of your crew and the secrets of the wider Expanse universe, and by doing so the paths that open to you can and will change.

Source: Xbox



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/03/26/the-expanse-osiris-reborn-is-coming-in-spring-2027-but-theres-a-closed-beta-next-month/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Rebellion reveals sci-fi action horror game Alien Deathstorm
item.content:encoded:
Rebellion, the UK studio best known for its Sniper Elite series, has revealed Alien Deathstorm, a sci-fi action horror game set to release in 2027 for PS5, Xbox Series X|S and PC – and day one into Xbox Game Pass.

Alien Deathstorm casts you as a Combat Engineer that has been dispatched to a remote off-world colony after a loss of communications. Arriving well ahead of a full rescue fleet, you job is to figure out what happened and try to save lives… but it very quickly becomes clear that the conditions on the planet have become nigh on impossible to survival, and then there’s the hostile alien monsters to worry about as well.

It looks pretty tasty from the reveal trailer, the man made facilities being ravaged by horrendously high winds and in a state of ruin, while the horror elements emerge from being hunted by alien creatures that seem to blend a little bit of Aliens and Predator in their design. There’s no hiding away from not showing them in order to raise suspense here, so take that for an indication of the action tone to the horror.

Alien Deathstorm is another sign of Rebellion trying to diversify their gaming portfolio. Sniper Elite has always been there for the studio, and the Zombie Army spin-off series has its schlocky horror appeal, but there was a period where it wasn’t quite certain how they could really branch out from that – Strange Brigade sadly wasn’t quite it. Thankfully last year’s Atomfall was a surprise hit, and Alien Deathstorm has the feel of something that could be similarly popular.

Source: press release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/03/26/rebellion-reveals-sci-fi-action-horror-game-alien-deathstorm/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
